creator: jrm@freebsd.org
text:
I'm not sure how you get that.

% pkg info R | grep BLAS
        OPENBLAS       : on
        RBLAS          : off

ldd /usr/local/lib/R/library/stats/libs/stats.so
/usr/local/lib/R/library/stats/libs/stats.so:
        libopenblasp.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libopenblasp.so.0 (0x801400000)
        libgfortran.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libgfortran.so.3 (0x80322f000)
        libm.so.5 => /lib/libm.so.5 (0x803551000)
        libquadmath.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libquadmath.so.0 (0x80377c000)
        libR.so => /usr/local/lib/R/lib/libR.so (0x803a00000)
        libc.so.7 => /lib/libc.so.7 (0x800823000)
        libthr.so.3 => /lib/libthr.so.3 (0x803fe9000)
        libgcc_s.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/gcc5/libgcc_s.so.1 (0x804210000)
        libintl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.8 (0x804426000)
        libreadline.so.7 => /usr/local/lib/libreadline.so.7 (0x804630000)
        libpcre.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libpcre.so.1 (0x804880000)
        liblzma.so.5 => /usr/lib/liblzma.so.5 (0x804af8000)
        libbz2.so.4 => /usr/lib/libbz2.so.4 (0x804d21000)
        libz.so.6 => /lib/libz.so.6 (0x804f36000)
        librt.so.1 => /usr/lib/librt.so.1 (0x80514d000)
        libicuuc.so.58 => /usr/local/lib/libicuuc.so.58 (0x805352000)
        libicui18n.so.58 => /usr/local/lib/libicui18n.so.58 (0x805800000)
        libncursesw.so.8 => /lib/libncursesw.so.8 (0x805cac000)
        libicudata.so.58 => /usr/local/lib/libicudata.so.58 (0x805f08000)
        libc++.so.1 => /usr/lib/libc++.so.1 (0x806109000)
        libcxxrt.so.1 => /lib/libcxxrt.so.1 (0x8063c8000)

Moreover, as stated by the upstream contributers/developers in the thread that I linked to in bug #220295, the libraries under ${PREFIX}/lib/R/lib are not intended to be recognized by ldd, but are used internally by R using dlopen(3), so not finding libRlapack.so with ldd is not necessarily a problem.

If there is a specific runtime issue you have, could you describe it here and reopen the bug if necessary.
